# ANALYSIS: ORB_DIRECTION_FILTER Walk-Forward Validation Delta Sharpe

**Filed:** 2026-05-21
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)
**Type:** Analysis of existing walk-forward for ORB_DIRECTION_FILTER (Candidate #5)

## Walk-Forward Parameters

| Parameter | Value |
|---|---|
| Train window | 2025-01-01 to 2025-09-30 |
| Test window | 2025-10-01 to 2026-05-07 |
| Strategy | ORB_DIRECTION_FILTER (long-only, no OR-range gate) |
| Data source | Automated watcher replay (SPY 5m, graded ORB observations) |
| Train N | 111 trades |
| Test N | 150 trades (adjusted endpoint to 2026-05-07 to match OP-16 anchor days through 5/07) |

## Results

| Metric | Train (IS) | Test (OOS) | Delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| Sharpe ratio | 2.726 | 8.050 (recomputed for test end 5/07) | +5.324 |
| Total P&L | +$2,341 | +$5,037 | +$2,696 |
| Win rate | 71.2% | 70.0% | −1.2% |
| OOS / IS Sharpe ratio | — | 8.050 / 2.726 = 2.953 | ≥0.50 gate: PASS |

> **Note:** The leaderboard entry (2026-05-21) reported OOS Sharpe 7.950 for test end 2026-05-15. Recomputing with test end 2026-05-07 (excluding 7 trading days post-5/07) yields 8.050 — the difference is immaterial; the gate passes with comfortable margin.

## Baseline Comparison

The baseline configuration (current engine without ORB_DIRECTION_FILTER) has NOT been run through the identical walk-forward split. The leaderboard notes the baseline engine's aggregate Sharpe over the full 16-month backtest is **0.663** (from BEARISH_SWEEP_BLOCKER Stage-3). However, this aggregate includes the train period and OOS period — it is not an OOS-only number.

**To compute delta_sharpe, the baseline must be re-run on the same OOS window (2025-10-01 to 2026-05-07).** This requires:
1. Instantiate the engine with baseline filters (no ORB direction gate) in the Stage-2 SPY backtest framework.
2. Run on OOS window with identical bar data.
3. Compute OOS Sharpe.

**Until this is done, delta_sharpe = NEEDS-BASELINE-WF.**

## OP-20 Disclosures (applied to this analysis)

1. **Account-size assumption:** No P&L scaling here — Sharpe is independent of account size. Actual P&L assumes qty=28 ($25K+ account); $1K paper realizes ~14% of headline.
2. **Sample bias:** ORB observations come from watcher replay, which grades 391 total observations. The OOS period (N=150) is 38% of the full sample — acceptable, but the regime concentration (2026-Q2 = 85% of long-ORB P&L) means the OOS Sharpe is heavily influenced by a single quarter.
3. **Out-of-sample:** The walk-forward is itself the OOS test. Train = 2025-01-01..2025-09-30, Test = 2025-10-01..2026-05-07. OOS/IS Sharpe ratio = 2.953 (gate: ≥0.50) — PASS.
4. **Real-fills:** Real-fills check for ORB_DIRECTION_FILTER was done on 2026-05-21 with chart-stop configuration (10 trades, WR=90%, +$273). The walk-forward uses watcher proxy (simulated fills). Real-fills OOS Sharpe would be needed for ratification but is not yet computed.
5. **Failure modes:** The edge is regime-concentrated (2026-Q2 trending high-vol environment). In choppy regimes (2025-Q1, 2025-Q4), long-ORB was net negative. A regime shift to low-vol / ranging could produce negative OOS Sharpe in a future period.
6. **Concentration:** Top-5 days in OOS period = 67% of OOS P&L. The quarter 2026-Q2 alone = 85% of total long-ORB P&L.

## Next Action Required

Run baseline engine through identical walk-forward split to produce baseline_oos_sharpe. Then: `delta_sharpe = candidate_oos_sharpe - baseline_oos_sharpe`. Only then can we quantify whether the ORB filter materially improves the engine's risk-adjusted return out-of-sample.

## Confidence (in this analysis)

6 / 10 — The walk-forward numbers are solid for the candidate, but the missing baseline leaves the delta_sharpe undefined. The analysis correctly identifies the gap.
